marketState: neutral, tilted bullish on weather with a bearish technical/storage overhang
summary: Front price ($2.909) sits almost exactly on modeled fair value ($2.917), so there is no mispricing edge to trade today. The weather picture is the standout signal: near-term demand is running +20.8% above the 5-year norm and the 8-16 day outlook is even hotter (+31.4%, above the 5-year max), which is fundamentally bullish for gas burn heading toward September, historically the strongest seasonal month. Working against that is a real supply overhang — storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average and last week's build surprised +72 Bcf bearish — plus every technical timeframe from 5-minute to weekly shows a bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses. Seasonality itself is unreliable right now (Low reliability, 59pp spread) and its typical 4-week move is slightly negative (-1.4%), while managed-money COT positioning is net short but only at the 35th percentile, i.e. not stretched. Net effect: a plausible multi-week bullish weather/seasonal story clashes with a bearish technical and storage backdrop, arguing for a small, patient long-term long and a wait-and-see stance short-term until price or technicals confirm.

longTerm.entry: $2.80 - $2.95 (current front ~$2.91, scale in on dips toward fair value)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; keep to 0.5-1R given Low seasonality reliability and the bearish storage surprise
longTerm.rationale: 8-16D weather demand is above the 5-year max heading into September (the strongest seasonal month per 16y history) and modeled fair value drifts up ~4% over the next 3 weeks ($2.917 -> $3.036), but the +184 Bcf storage surplus and a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise cap conviction.
longTerm.invalidation: A larger-than-forecast storage build/surprise, a fade in the 8-16D heat forecast back toward normal, or a decisive break below the $2.60 area invalidates the long thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Price is essentially at fair value (front spread just -$0.008, status FAIR) so there's no near-term mispricing edge, while all six technical timeframes (5m-1w) show a bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses, directly conflicting with the hot 1-7D weather (+20.8% vs 5y) demand signal.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) technical confirmation - price reclaims the 1h/4h 50-EMA with MACD turning positive or RSI(14) pushing above 55 - confirming the hot weather is showing up in price, or (b) a dip toward the $2.65-$2.75 fair-value support zone to buy the extreme 8-16D heat forecast (rangePos 1.23, above 5y max) at a discount.

keyDrivers: 8-16D demand forecast +31.4% vs 5y and above the 5-year max (rangePos 1.23); 1-7D also hot (+20.8%, rangePos 0.83)
Front price is at fair value ($2.909 vs $2.917 fair) while modeled fair value drifts up ~4% over the next 3 weeks
Storage surplus +184 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise last report
All six technical timeframes (5m-1w) show a bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses
Seasonality reliability is Low (59pp 10-90 spread); September is historically the strongest month, approaching within the long-term window
risks: Seasonality reliability is Low, so the seasonal edge (and the -1.4% typical 4-week move) carries weak statistical confidence
Persistent storage surplus/bearish surprises could keep a lid on price even with hot weather
Technical trend is bearish across every timeframe, so a longer-term long is entered against the prevailing near-term trend
